architectural_patterns.explanation: The system follows a client-server model where the client (user platform) makes requests to a server (software processing requests) over the internet.
The system is divided into three logical layers: Presentation Layer (visual interface), Business Logic Layer (processing information), and Data Layer (storage).

The business logic layer is composed of multiple independent services (Api Gateway, Core, Analytics Module, Api Player, Web Socket) that interact with each other to improve security, scalability, and availability.
A single entry point (Api Gateway) handles all client requests, managing authentication, authorization, SSL, and routing to internal microservices.
A centralized repository (using AWS services like S3) stores structured and unstructured data at scale without requiring modification or structuring beforehand, allowing for analysis.
Data transmission from producers (microservices) to consumers (storage/analysis) is handled via streaming services (Kinesis Data Streams) for near real-time processing.

role.explanation: Visual interface of the Waapiti platform where users interact with the system.

technical_details.explanation: Web and mobile interfaces communicating via HTTP protocol.

communication.explanation: Communicates with the Api Gateway component via HTTP.

role.explanation: Manages, authenticates, and authorizes users; defines REST API routes and processes requests to send to Core and Analytics Module.

communication.explanation: Receives HTTP requests from clients; communicates with Core, Analytics Module, and Players via TCP.

role.explanation: Main business logic of the platform; interacts primarily with the database and cloud services.

technical_details.explanation: NestJS microservice handling platform actions (create, edit, delete entities).

communication.explanation: Interacts with Api Gateway (via TCP), Database, and sends data to Kinesis Data Streams for Audit Trail.

role.explanation: Obtains and processes data from playlogs, connections, and platform actions for analysis.

technical_details.explanation: NestJS microservice developed specifically for this project, interacting with AWS Athena and Database.

communication.explanation: Receives requests from Api Gateway; queries Amazon Athena and Database; returns results to Api Gateway.
Receives information from Players; processes and stores data.

role.explanation: Enables bidirectional communication between the platform and players for information exchange.

communication.explanation: Processes data from players; stores data in Database and Cloud Services; generates Playlog data sent to Kinesis.

role.explanation: Stores all necessary data for system functioning.

technical_details.explanation: Relational database implemented in AWS RDS service.

communication.explanation: Interacted with by Core and Analytics Module services.

role.explanation: Object storage service forming part of the Data Lake to store historical documents (JSON).

technical_details.explanation: Stores JSON files organized by date, client, and user folders.

communication.explanation: Receives data from Kinesis Data Firehose; queried by Amazon Athena.

role.explanation: Non-relational database storing playlogs in real-time.

technical_details.explanation: Uses Composite Key (Partition Key: player ID, Sort Key: date/time).

communication.explanation: Used by Core/Web Socket for real-time storage.

role.explanation: Data ingestion and processing service for streaming data (Playlogs and Audit Trail).

technical_details.explanation: Generators are NestJS microservices (WebSocket, Core); Consumers are Kinesis Data Firehose.

communication.explanation: Receives data from Core and Web Socket; sends data to Kinesis Data Firehose.

role.explanation: Loads large amounts of data into data stores (S3).

technical_details.explanation: Configured to load data every 60 seconds or when reaching 5MB; performs dynamic partitioning.

communication.explanation: Reads from Kinesis Data Streams; writes to Amazon S3.

role.explanation: Serverless query service allowing SQL queries on data stored in S3.

technical_details.explanation: Executes parallel queries on JSON/CSV/Parquet formats; uses partitions for efficiency.

communication.explanation: Queried by Analytics Module to retrieve data from S3.

role.explanation: Serverless data integration service used for metadata storage (Data Catalog).

technical_details.explanation: Stores metadata about data location and table definitions including partitions.

communication.explanation: Works in conjunction with Athena to read data correctly from S3.

design_patterns.explanation: NestJS Providers (Services) are designed to be injected as dependencies into Modules or other components to manage business logic and external service connections.
Classes responsible for receiving HTTP requests and processing them, delegating logic to providers/services.
Classes containing the business logic of the application, imported into modules and used by controllers.
Uses the @Cron decorator from @nestjs/schedule to execute functions periodically (e.g., obtaining connections every hour).
Uses @UseGuards decorator to perform validation and permission checks before accessing endpoint functions.
